title: The Magus Who Followed the Star
narration: I am a Magus, a watcher of the heavens, schooled in the courses of the stars.
We are no workers of magic — we are called great in wisdom, the firstfruits of the faith.
From the East we come, from the lands of the Persians, the Medes, the Chaldeans.
I knew this light was no ordinary star.
Its substance is not the incorruptible fire of the firmament — it burns from a corruptible thing, made low, near to the earth.
It does not wheel from East to West as the others do. It runs straight, from the rising toward the south.
It is no larger than two or three cubits, and it hovers in the air, not far above us.
When we walk, it walks. When we stop and rest, it rests and waits over us.
It shines at noonday, in the full blaze of the sun, and its own splendor overcomes even the sun's rays.
I know now what no astronomy taught me: this is the sign foretold by Balaam — a star shall rise out of Jacob.
Divine inspiration opens my mind: this light does not mark seasons or years. It marks the birth of the Creator.
We seek One who is God and man, and the star tells us only this — the Savior is born.
We enter Jerusalem — and the star hides itself from us.
We leave Herod behind, and it shows itself again, going before us.
It leads us to the place. Here. A stable. A poor Mother. A child.
I fall down. I adore Him.
I open my treasures: gold for His poverty, to sustain a poor Mother and Son.
Frankincense against the stench of this stable, against the foulness of this place.
Myrrh for the tenderness of an infant's body, to strengthen His weak small limbs.
I came by the way of darkness. I will go home by another way.
prayer: O good Jesus, born of the Virgin, who revealed yourself to the Magi with the star for guide.
Let the light of your grace shine into the darkness of my conscience.
By your glad appearing, give me full knowledge of you and of myself, that within I may see you, within I may find you.
There let me offer to your majesty the myrrh of inward compunction, the incense of devout prayer, the gold of loving devotion.
I went out from the homeland of supernal joy by the way of darkness and of fault.
Lead me back, by the way of truth and grace, to that country, with you to guide me. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: qui de Virgine natus teipsum Magis, stella duce, revelasti, et eos per aliam viam in regionem suam redire fecisti
latinJewel.english: who, born of the Virgin, revealed yourself to the Magi with the star for guide, and made them return into their own country by another way
